TextENGNew: I can't remember when things got this bad.
No -- I don't even want to bother trying to remember. I could think of more than ten possible turning points, but I had no desire to trace back to where it all started going wrong.
Every last one of them, worthless. Just recalling my family's faces fills me with contempt, and I couldn't care less what's become of them.
Every woman is an enemy, always ready to devour or be devoured. Every man who came sniffing around was unbearably dull -- having them at my side wasn't just boring, it was revolting.
It makes my blood boil. Why are the people around me so useless and stupid? Every time they open their mouths, all they talk about is themselves, hurling their desires at me.
No matter how many sweet, flattering words they pile up, it's all just garbage tossed into the stinking gutter of ulterior motives.
So no matter how prettily their words glittered, they'd sink and vanish in an instant. I was sick of it.
I spat on the ground. My mouth had tasted acrid and foul for a while now, and it was irritating.
I looked around for something to drink, but of course there were no vending machines on this backwater country road. That only pissed me off more, and I felt like screaming at the top of my lungs.
I haven't done anything wrong. Everyone else is to blame.
By now, I was supposed to be settling into a luxury apartment in Kokura, lounging on a brand-new sofa with a drink in hand.
Or sitting in first class on an international flight, counting my stacks of cash.
Either way, just thinking about the people who ruined my guaranteed happiness made my blood boil.
``Tch... 'Family'?! What a load of crap!@k@vS14/20/212100029. Don't give me that bullshit, you moron!!``
Remembering that pathetic middle-aged man Ryuugu's words, I screamed at nobody in particular.
After everything I gave him, he suddenly cut off all contact with me.
When I started getting suspicious, a lawyer claiming to be his 'representative' showed up to notify me of a unilateral cancellation of our engagement.
And to top it all off, the lawyer had the Sonozakis' backing...
When that bastard started spouting nonsense, I screamed at him and demanded compensation -- but then he brought up Tetsu, and told me to settle things 'amicably.'
``Damn it!@k@vS14/20/212100031. Aaagh, shit! This pisses me off!!``
It's not like I ever cared about Tetsu. He was stupid, loud but spineless, and on top of that, a lazy good-for-nothing who did nothing but laze around...
Even though he barely did anything this time, he had the nerve to demand an even split. That's why I'd already decided to cut ties with him once I squeezed the money out of old man Ryuugu.
...And yet that pathetic waste of space went and blew all the money meant for my apartment!!
In a fit of rage, I smashed the whiskey bottle in my hand against a telephone pole.
The glass shattered with a spectacular crash, and the rich, pungent smell of whiskey filled the air.
...There had been quite a bit left, and wasting it like that -- realizing this was all I had to show for my trouble -- only made me angrier.
...The transfer notice came out of nowhere.
A robbery targeting the Sonozaki treasury. I'd planned it meticulously, painstakingly managed to copy the key, and the money was practically in my hands.
...Sneaking into the office went smoothly, but I couldn't get into the crucial safe, and the whole plan fell apart.
The key to the safe room... didn't fit.
Impossible. After all that meticulous planning, after risking my life if I were caught -- was my prized key actually worthless?!
...Naturally, the crew directed their fury and disappointment at me, since I'd been responsible for obtaining the key. We traded insults and blame, and our makeshift gang dissolved on the spot.
But my frustration was far from over.
I figured I'd at least make them pay for running their mouths at me, so I went straight to the general manager, Kasai, and ratted out the whole robbery plan.
Of course, I kept my own involvement out of it. ...But Kasai gave me a thin smile, then slowly drew a handgun and pointed it at me.

``...Enough with the act.@k@vS14/15/2115000077. Did you seriously think we didn't know?@k@vS14/15/2115000078. Consider it a mercy that I'm letting you keep your job.@k@vS14/15/2115000079. Now get lost.``
changeReason: Stylistic rephrasing throughout; both old and new convey all four lines of dialogue with the same core meaning.

TextENGNew: Then the general manager transferred me from my current bar to a second-rate dump. Naturally, the new place was cheaper, dingier... a dead-end joint that only attracted penny-pinching lowlifes.
``Aaahh!!@k@vS14/20/212100033. Shit, shit, shit, god damn it!!!``

Just thinking about it made me furious. I couldn't say a word against Kasai's gun and his piercing gaze, and the humiliation of cowering in fear and scrambling away like a pathetic wretch was unbearable.
Being wealthy and independent had been right within my grasp, yet here I was -- nothing but a washed-up drunk dumped at some second-rate bar.

A violent wave of nausea surged up my throat, and I spewed everything onto the roadside. Grimacing at the sour stench of stomach acid, I watched the filth seep into the ground before me, and suddenly found it funny.

...What a pathetic sight. This is the price for trying to crawl out of a rotten, gutter-soaked life. Some religious nut would probably call it divine punishment for a lowlife who dared to dream of happiness.
But what's wrong with that? What's wrong with wanting to be happy?
I've done everything I could. Being a woman fighting to survive in this cruel world -- how many times have I been forced to sell pieces of myself?
changeReason: OLD generalizes to "women"; JPN and NEW correctly use first person "how many times have I been forced to sell pieces of myself."

TextENGNew: So none of this is my fault. The ones to blame are the people who drove me to this.
Family, friends, acquaintances, all of them. Why wasn't I blessed with even the bare minimum of luck when it came to the people around me? ...Obviously, it's their fault.
When I looked up, there was a clinic right in front of me.
I was in no mood to see Tetsu's obnoxious face. Wanting to get away from Okinomiya, I'd just been walking aimlessly -- and before I knew it, I'd ended up here in Hinamizawa.

The clinic was dark inside and utterly silent, naturally closed for the night. In the darkness, I could only make out the vague outline of the building, and its eerie atmosphere made me shiver slightly.
However.
At the same time, a thought struck me. This was an opportunity I couldn't have asked for.
A clinic means... doctors, obviously. They sell cheap medicine at outrageous prices and rake in massive insurance payouts from the government.
I had no idea how much a rural clinic brought in, but they had to be sitting on far more savings than your average villager.
Especially that pale-faced weakling of a young doctor, Irie or whatever his name was. I'd seen him driving around Okinomiya in an expensive sports car, and his clothes always looked pricey too.
Even a moment's thought made it clear he was pulling in a decent salary.
And if that was the case... this clinic surely had a nice stash of money hidden somewhere.
``Heh heh heh...@k@vS14/20/212100037. I've got nothing left to lose...!``
A clinic's security would be nothing special. Even if I failed and got arrested, I'd just serve a few years in prison and be out.
I could figure out what to do after that once I got out. By then, things in Okinomiya would have blown over anyway.
And above all, if this went well, I'd get my hands on a considerable sum -- maybe not as much as the Sonozaki treasury, but still...!
``Kyahahahaha~, what a brilliant idea~!``
How funny, really. Unlike the Sonozakis, there was zero risk to my life here.
Weighing risk against reward, the better bet was obvious. I couldn't fathom why none of those idiots I'd worked with had ever thought of it.
``Now then...``
I crept silently toward the clinic and surveyed the perimeter.
...The front entrance was out of the question from the start. It was hard to imagine it being unlocked, and with no tools, forcing it open would be impossible.
That meant the obvious choice was a window.
Windows are actually a surprisingly overlooked vulnerability. Since air conditioning can sometimes bother patients, clinics tend to open and close their windows regularly for ventilation.
And it's common for someone to accidentally forget to lock one, or leave a screen window in place.
I cautiously approached a window and tried rattling it to see if it was locked.
...No luck.
The next one over. ...Also no luck.

After repeating this several times, I started to think the houses were far enough apart that I might as well just smash the glass with a rock -- and at that very moment, one window slid open without the slightest resistance.
``Hehehe... my lucky day...!``
Stifling the laugh that threatened to bubble up, I grabbed the window frame with both hands and pulled myself up.
I managed to slide my upper body through, but when I tried to pull my legs up -- maybe because of the alcohol -- they wouldn't cooperate, and I tumbled inside.
I cursed reflexively, but a grin quickly spread across my face. My body ached all over and I might have scraped something, but in my state of elation, I couldn't have cared less.
``Now then... where's the treasure...?``
``--Tch! What a stingy place...!``
I finally found a coin purse in one of the desk drawers and stuffed it into my pocket, then kicked a chair in frustration.
It made a dull sound as it rolled on its casters to the corner of the room, hitting the wall and toppling over.
After hours of ransacking what appeared to be an examination room, all I'd found was a single coin purse stuffed with hundred-yen and ten-yen coins.
...Obviously, that wasn't going to cut it. This pocket change would be gone after two days of eating and drinking.
``I didn't risk sneaking in here just to settle for this...!``
I opened the examination room door and stepped into the hallway. In the dim glow of a security light, I could make out several doors leading to other rooms.
Among them, my instinct told me the door at the end of the hallway smelled of money. Trusting that hunch, I put my hand on the doorknob and slowly, carefully turned it.
...The door opened easily. Beyond it lay a staircase leading up to the second floor, with cardboard boxes piled high in the back.
I picked up a box to check what was inside, and behind it -- in a spot hard to notice at first glance -- I found a staircase leading down to a basement.
``This is getting more and more promising... hehe!``
I felt no fear as I peered into the gloom below -- my head was too high on adrenaline. I stepped forward cautiously, descending the stairs one step at a time.
``......What the hell is this?``
I stood there, dumbfounded. To my astonishment, the basement was as spacious as the first floor above, with the same general layout and multiple rooms.
The Irie Clinic already had two floors and plenty of rooms. There was even open land around it. There should have been no need to build rooms underground.
And yet.
Going to the trouble of building hidden rooms underground instead of simply expanding above could only mean one thing -- something extremely important was being concealed down here.
...With that understanding, I eagerly reached for the nearest doorknob and opened it.
The moment I did, a harsh chemical smell assaulted my senses and made me wince, but what I saw inside the dimly lit room was --
Shelf after shelf of specimens sealed in glass tubes.
An enormous tank... big enough to fit not just a person, but a hippopotamus inside.
And connected to it, complex equipment resembling an oscilloscope quietly emitted periodic electronic beeps, as if monitoring or recording data.
No -- looking more closely, I could see multiple cylinders standing inside the tank. I peered inside one to see what it contained and -- I gasped. Those were... human brains, and... spinal cords...?!
``N-no way... is that... real...?!``
And then, finally.
I realized... I had stumbled into someplace truly terrifying.
Every drop of blood in my body seemed to freeze into ice, sending a dreadful chill coursing through me. The giddy drunken buzz that had carried me this far drained away in an instant.
Run.
Get out of here. Now.
I thought that and took a step back -- but I knocked something off a desk. When I heard the dull clatter and looked down at my feet, my eyes went wide with horror.
It was -- unmistakably -- a handgun. Not the kind gangsters use. One look told me it was military-grade...!
``Wh-what is this place...?!``
``...Oh my, it's you.@k@vS14/09/210900036. I came to see what kind of rat had gotten in... and this is what I find. How disappointing.``
I caught my breath and spun around. Standing there was a woman in a nurse's uniform.
...I'd seen her before. Takano, that was it. The nurse who works at this clinic.

``A mountain labored and brought forth a mouse... a sewer rat, at that.@k@vS14/09/210900038. I let you wander around to see if you'd prove interesting, but it seems you're just a common thief.@k@vS14/09/210900039. How terribly boring.``
Calmly, unhurriedly, her eyes fixed on me, Takano approached -- one slow, deliberate step at a time.
Her face wore a gentle smile, her manner soft and composed... but there wasn't a single opening.
And above all, her eyes, illuminated in the dim room, held absolutely no trace of emotion. Cold as glass marbles, they simply locked onto their target with unblinking, unwavering focus.
Those aren't human eyes. No -- nothing with eyes like that is human...!
``S-s-s-stop right there!@k@vS14/20/212100053. D-don't come any closer!!``

Desperately, I snatched up the military handgun from the floor and aimed it at the nurse. My hands trembled violently, but I gripped the handle with both hands and curled my finger around the trigger.
``Oh my... hehe.@k@vS14/09/210900041. What a dangerous toy you've found.``
Seeing the gun, Takano's eyes widened slightly in surprise and she stopped. But then her smile only deepened into a wicked grin.

...The more she smiled, the more the evil radiating from her eyes, her lips, her entire being intensified -- as if I were facing the devil incarnate, an overwhelming, absolute terror seized my entire body.

``Every one of these laboratories is a critically important facility.@k@vS14/09/210900043. A tremendous amount of cutting-edge scientific technology has been invested in them, generously and without restraint.``

``And that tank behind you, by the way, is one of the most expensive items here.@k@vS14/09/210900045. It is nothing less than a sacred altar for the ritual of deepening human knowledge -- an offering to God.``
``A ritual... to God...?!``
``Yes, that's right.@k@vS14/09/210900047. Those who could not, unfortunately, continue their lives as humans have offered their power to God, opening the path to Shangri-La.``
``There, there is no discrimination, no suffering, no hatred -- a place where one may freely and eternally cultivate and savor their desires by their own will...``
``Doesn't that sound like an ideal world?``
Takano spread her arms wide as if the gun before her didn't exist, gazing up at the ceiling in rapture as she spoke. The sheer incomprehensibility of her words made her all the more terrifying.
``Sh-shut up!@k@vS14/20/212100056. I'm not listening, and I don't want to hear it!``
``Oh... how disappointing.@k@vS14/09/210900051. I thought it would make a lovely bedtime story.@k@vS14/09/210900052. ...For King Enma's court.``
It happened in an instant.
Takano swiftly drew a handgun from behind her back and fired. I crumpled to the floor.
Something hot and slippery gushed endlessly from my right upper arm, and I felt consciousness rapidly slipping away.
``...Ow... shit... damn it...!``

``Hee hee hee... that won't do.@k@vS14/09/210900054. Didn't anyone teach you to fire as soon as you draw?@k@vS14/09/210900055. Well, that gun wasn't even loaded, so it would've ended the same way regardless...?``
Looking down at my writhing, agonized form with evident amusement, she grabbed me by the hair and yanked me up.
Then, bringing her cruelly twisted smile close to my face, she blew warm breath into my ear and whispered in a voice so cold, so calm, so utterly devoid of feeling.
``Since you've come all this way, why not enjoy yourself...@k@vS14/09/210900057. You see, I happen to be in the middle of researching a truly wonderful new drug.``
``The ultimate weapon, beyond anything that's ever existed...@k@vS14/09/210900059. Doesn't that sound wonderful?@k@vS14/09/210900060. Hee hee hee...``
Those were the last words I heard in this world as a 'human.'
Suddenly, the woman's eyes rolled back, all strength left her body, and she crumpled to the floor unconscious. Foam dripped from her slack mouth, and her expression was almost comically absurd.
``...Oh dear, I wasn't finished talking yet.@k@vS14/09/210900063. Hee hee hee...@k@vS14/09/210900064. Well, a fine pre-experiment warm-up, I suppose.``
Just then, I sensed someone behind me and turned around. A stern-faced man in a military uniform stood at the entrance to the room with a grave expression.
...It was Okonogi. The commander of the 'Mountain Dogs,' the security unit for this research facility, operating directly under my command.
``...How is the security system performing?@k@vS14/09/210900066. Was it working properly?``

``The specified functions seem to be operating as intended.@k@vS14/14/213400002. Infrared and acoustic intruder detection, along with the alert relay to the guard station, all performed as expected.``
``...However, having all the systems under centralized management could be a fatal vulnerability from a security standpoint.``
``Oh? What do you mean?``
``In short, if the core system goes down, everything gets bypassed.@k@vS14/14/213400005. ...That said, the only people who can disable it are you and the director, so it should be fine.``
changeReason: OLD says "Major or higher"; NEW says "you and the director" -- different specificity about system access.

TextENGNew: ``So you're saying if the director or I wanted to rob this place, we could waltz right through?@k@vS14/09/210900069. ...Hee hee. In that case, next time I'll play the burglar and test the infiltration. Would you indulge me?``
Okonogi shrugged and gave a wry laugh at my joke. Then he looked down at the foolish female rat lying at my feet.
``...Are you sure she won't be a problem?``
``Not at all.@k@vS14/09/210900071. Apparently this woman tried to steal from the Sonozaki Group's treasury.@k@vS14/09/210900072. So they'll be only too happy to clean things up on that end.``
``Well, I suppose sneaking in here was where her luck ran out.``
With that, Okonogi gave the woman's head a light tap with the toe of his boot. The impact wasn't nearly enough to wake her -- the only sign of life was the faint rise and fall of her breathing.

``...All right, we begin at the designated location in two hours.@k@vS14/09/210900074. Make sure all experiment participants have completed their preventive medication.@k@vS14/09/210900075. Have this rat prepped and ready as well.``
changeReason: OLD says "test subject" (singular); JPN means "experiment participants" (plural); NEW also drops the laugh and adds a different line.

TextENGNew: ...On his way back from a cigarette break in the rest area, Ooishi happened to notice a family entering through the public entrance.
He recognized one of them immediately -- it was a man he knew well. Akasaka.
``Well, well!@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1002. If it isn't the whole Akasaka family, here in person!@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1003. Nfufufu~, welcome, welcome~!``
``Ah, Ooishi-san!@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1002. Sorry... I couldn't resist coming by.``
``Hello, Uncle Ooishi~!!``
The girl between Akasaka and the woman who was presumably his wife -- Miyuki -- bounced up and down with a beaming smile, waving enthusiastically at Ooishi.
So this is what it's like to have grandchildren, he thought. ...With a tender smile that couldn't have been further from his usual lecherous grin, he waved back.
``Ohhh, Miyuki-chan!@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1005. You still remember me?@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1006. I'm so happy I could cry~!``
``Ahahaha!@k@vS14/34/VT4Bc_miyuki1003. That's because Uncle Ooishi is kind, just like Grandpa on Mama's side♪@k@vS14/34/VT4Bc_miyuki1004. Right~, Mama?``
``Now, Miyuki.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1002. He's an important friend of Papa's, so mind your manners.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1003. I'm sorry about this. She's at that age, you know...``
changeReason: OLD captures JPN about being looked after previously; NEW replaces with "She's at that age" losing the specific reference.

TextENGNew: After gently chiding the excited Miyuki, the woman bowed gracefully to Ooishi.
...She had such a lovely presence that even the nearby staff couldn't help but turn their heads.
``N-no no, not at all!@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1008. ...So, you must be Akasaka-san's wife?@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1009. Veeery nice to meet you, I'm Ooishi!``
``Oh, forgive me for not introducing myself sooner... I'm Akasaka's wife.``
``Nfufufu, I've heard so much about you~!@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1011. ...But still, hearing about someone and seeing them in person are two completely different things!``
``Your daughter is so adorable that I figured her mother must be beautiful -- but you far exceed even my imagination!``
``You wouldn't happen to be a model back in Tokyo, would you?``
``N-no... that's too much...!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1006. Someone like me could never do such a job...!``
``...Ooishi-san, please stop flirting with my wife.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1004. How many women have you used that line on, exactly?``
``Nahahaha!@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1015. How rude -- I meant every word!``
``Honestly, Akasaka-san, I envy you!@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1017. If a beauty like her were married to anyone else, I'd be at the shrine tonight praying for divine intervention!``
changeReason: OLD captures the specific cultural reference of nailing straw dolls (五寸釘を藁人形に); NEW changes to generic "praying for divine intervention."

TextENGNew: Ooishi laughed heartily, his belly shaking. Akasaka smiled back warmly, clearly trusting him completely.
Meanwhile, Yukie gently tugged at her husband's sleeve and tilted her head with a meaningful glance. Akasaka nodded in understanding and ushered her forward.
``I'm sorry to bother you during work hours.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1006. I was actually planning to come alone, but my wife insisted on paying her respects.``
``Oh? To... me?``

``Yes.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1008. I heard you saved my husband from a dangerous situation five years ago... Thank you so very much.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1010. My daughter and I owe you an incredible debt that we could never repay.``
changeReason: NEW replaces the JPN last sentence about apologizing for the late greeting with "My daughter and I owe you an incredible debt."

TextENGNew: ``Nahahaha, think nothing of it!@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1021. It's a detective's code -- you never forget when someone saves your life, but when you're the one who saved someone, you let it go~.@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1022. Please, don't worry about it!``
...It was the kidnapping of the Minister of Construction's grandson, five years ago.
During a struggle with the kidnapper, Akasaka had found himself in a desperate situation, and it was Ooishi's assistance that saved him.
And then... it suddenly hit him.@k This girl, Miyuki, had been in her mother's womb back then...
Thinking about that... Ooishi felt genuinely glad he'd stepped in to help that day.
If anything had happened to Akasaka back then, this moment -- meeting his wife and daughter, all of them smiling together -- would never have existed...
``Hmm? ...Oh, sorry, we should get going.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1008. Excuse us, Ooishi-san. We're heading to...``
``That's not what I meant... listen, dear.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1012. Why don't Miyuki and I go ahead to the inn?``

``I'm sure you and Ooishi-san have plenty of catching up to do, right?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1014. He's someone important to you that you rarely get to see -- isn't that fine?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1015. Miyuki and I will relax at the hot springs.``
``Wh-what are you saying...?@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1011. This is a family trip -- I can't just do that.``
``She's right, ma'am!@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1024. I wouldn't dream of intruding on your family time.@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1025. I'm just glad I got to meet you like this!``

``No, really, it's fine.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1017. He's always talking about you at home... He was so looking forward to seeing you today.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1018. It would be a shame to waste that.``
``So, I apologize for making such a presumptuous request when you must be so busy... but would you please spend some time with my husband?``
``But, what about you two...``
``We'll be fine.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1020. I checked the station map earlier, and it's not far by train.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1021. So we can head over on our own with no trouble at all.``
With the distance between them and their busy schedules, they rarely had a chance to meet and talk. Yukie must have seen how the two men lit up in each other's company and decided to step aside.
She had probably looked up the train routes earlier with exactly this possibility in mind.
Truly a wonderful wife. ...Ooishi watched their exchange with warm, admiring eyes.
``Huh...?@k@vS14/34/VT4Bc_miyuki1006. Papa, you're not coming to the hot spring?``
``Hehe... you'll be together later.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1023. But Miyuki, Papa's done lots of things with you recently, hasn't he?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1024. Now it's Mama's turn!``
``Miyuki, won't you have fun at the hot spring with just Mama?``
``Of course I will!@k@vS14/34/VT4Bc_miyuki1006b. Miyuki loves both Mama and Papa sooo much!``
``Miyuki...``
In truth, Akasaka himself felt that a brief greeting would have been terribly inadequate.
But... he'd come here for his family, and the fact that they were the ones being considerate for his sake made him both grateful and guilty.
``Thank you, ma'am!@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1028. Well then, Akasaka-san, your wife's gone to all this trouble.@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1029. Let's make the most of her thoughtfulness!``
As if to sweep away Akasaka's hesitation, Ooishi let out a hearty laugh and gave him a firm slap on the back.
That seemed to settle it. Akasaka made up his mind and responded with a resolute nod.
``...You're right.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1016. Sorry, Yukie -- just for tonight, I'll take you up on your kindness.``
``Of course, don't worry about us.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1029. You can come back as late as tomorrow morning if you'd like. Please take your time.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1030. Well then...``
``R-right...``
But... that final smile of hers -- her eyes weren't smiling.

``You've gotten quite a bit stronger at drinking, Akasaka-san.@k@vS14/11/210700027. I'm starting to think I can't beat you anymore.@k@vS14/11/210700028. It really is true that you can't hold your drink as well when you get old...!``
changeReason: Both say Ooishi admits can't hold drink as well when old - same content, minor rephrasing.

TextENGNew: ``You flatter me.@k@vS14/13/211300011. The only reason I can handle my liquor is because an intelligence officer can't be a lightweight.@k@vS14/13/211300012. I've trained in all sorts of drinking establishments.``
``Sake, whiskey, shaoxing wine!@k@vS14/13/211300014. Sometimes it's a little bar under a noren curtain, sometimes it's a place with mirror balls!``
``Nahahahahaha!!@k@vS14/11/210700030. That's the spirit!@k@vS14/11/210700031. You'll have to take me along sometime!``
``Please do come teach the younger officers.@k@vS14/13/211300016. These days, they're always whining about how they 'just can't drink' from birth.``
``I couldn't drink at all when I started either.@k@vS14/13/211300018. The more you drink, the stronger you get, and the more you come to appreciate the flavor!``
``Hear, hear!!!@k@vS14/11/210700033. Are you taking notes, Kuma-chan?``
``Y-yes, sir...@k@vS14/19/213100002. I've actually gotten a lot better!``
``Wahahahaha!!@k@vS14/00/213000002. That's right. When you first got paired with Ooishi, your toast was with oolong tea!``
``That won't do!@k@vS14/13/211300020. The opening toast carries real significance.``
``Refusing to drink alcohol at the toast is basically saying you object to the whole gathering.``
``So whenever I see young people pulling that, I set them straight!@k@vS14/13/211300023. I'm not saying chug it!@k@vS14/13/211300024. But at least wet your lips out of respect for the occasion!``

The Akasaka who had first visited Okinomiya years ago had been stiff with nerves. But the man before them now showed none of that. ...The confidence that came from years of being forged in the field -- Ooishi could feel it.
``Nahahahahaha!!@k@vS14/11/210700035. Now that's style, Akasaka-san!!!``
``My, you've really become someone to be reckoned with!``

``In just the few years I've been counting down to retirement, you've grown this much.@k@vS14/11/210700038. They say old soldiers should fade away, and seeing someone like you makes me think maybe it's time!``
``Not at all!@k@vS14/13/211300026. If anything, I've learned just how much experience matters in this line of work.``
``Leave the legwork and crime scenes to us young guys. Veterans like you should be sitting proudly at their desks, putting us to work!``
``Did you hear that, Kuma-chaaaan?!``
``Ow, ow ow ow, I'm sorry, I'm sorry!!!@k@vS14/19/213100004. I'll work harder!!``
changeReason: OLD translates いてててて (pain expression) as "Okay, okayyyy! I heard him!!" losing the pain; NEW correctly renders as "Ow, ow ow ow."

TextENGNew: ``Wahahahaha!@k@vS14/00/213000004. Ooishi, Kumagai-kun is already doing his best, you know.@k@vS14/00/213000005. It's you, the lazy one, who should be giving him more credit!``

``Ooishi-san is quite the handful, isn't he?@k@vS14/13/211300028_029_01. I only worked with him for a few days, but I understand exactly how you feel, Kumagai-san.@k@vS14/13/211300028_029_02. There's no keeping up with him.``
``But trust me, Ooishi-san will make you into a fine officer.@k@vS14/13/211300028_029_03. Believe in him and follow him to the end.``
``Y-yes, sir! I'll do my best!!@k@vS14/19/213100006. But even if I can learn to drink, mahjong is beyond me!!@k@vS14/19/213100007. Please spare me from that!!``

``Nonsense, mahjong is all about experience too!@k@vS14/13/211300030. The more you play, the more skill and confidence you build!@k@vS14/13/211300031. We'll play a round later, so you'd better be ready!``
``...Ooishi, he's a good man.@k@vS14/00/213000010. Reminds me a bit of you back in your reckless days!``
``Oh, come now!@k@vS14/11/210700041. I was never this handsome!@k@vS14/11/210700042. Nfufufu!``
Those who knew Ooishi well had never seen him welcome anyone with such genuine delight.
To Ooishi, Akasaka was merely someone he'd worked with for a few days years ago. They'd exchanged New Year's cards and the like since then, but that had been the extent of their relationship.
Yet Ooishi had never known how gratifying it could be to see a once-green rookie return all grown up.

Ooishi had always intended to work until the very end. But seeing a junior officer develop into someone so capable, he began to think that stepping aside to watch from retirement might not be so bad after all.
Ooishi and Akasaka savored their reunion over drinks far more than either of them had expected...
The shop owner was bringing the electric sign inside from out front.
The chairs at every other table had been stacked on top, and his wife was giving the floor a light mopping.
All the other customers were long gone. The clock was approaching three in the morning.
``This is a reunion, right, Kura-chan?@k@vS14/00/master10002. Take your time.@k@vS14/00/master10003. I'll leave the back door unlocked, so just pull it shut when you leave.``
``Sorry to be such a bother.``
At this hour, there was nowhere else to go. With so much left to talk about, Ooishi and the others gratefully accepted the owner's hospitality.
Kumagai had lost his battle against drowsiness and was bobbing his head in and out of sleep.

The conversation had turned to Akasaka's current work. He belonged to a particularly unusual division within Public Safety. His tales of battle-like days and dangerous tightrope walks had thoroughly captivated Ooishi and the others.
``...Actually, one of the reasons I came here was that I wanted to meet a girl named Furude Rika.``

``Aah... come to think of it, you mentioned something about that before.@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1032. But Akasaka-san, what business could you possibly have with the Furude family's only surviving child?``
``Only surviving...?@k@vS14/13/211300034. ...So her parents are...?``

Furude Rika had once predicted to Akasaka that bizarre incidents would occur in Hinamizawa for five consecutive years. Among those predictions, the third year -- if he recalled correctly -- included the death of her own parents.
``Yeah, that was the third year of Oyashiro-sama's curse. Hmmm, 1981, I believe.@k@vS14/11/210700046. The Furude couple passed away under rather suspicious circumstances.``
``...Three years later...?!@k@vS14/13/211300036. Did it happen in June, three years after I visited Hinamizawa?!``
``That's right.@k@vS14/11/210700049. ...What's the matter?@k@vS14/11/210700050. Something on your mind?``
Akasaka squeezed his eyes shut, trying to force the alcohol from his brain, and struggled to recall every detail of the girl's prophecy.
...That's right -- this was exactly what he'd come here to confirm.
``I came here in 1978.@k@vS14/13/211300038. The year after that, what happened to the dam construction foreman?@k@vS14/13/211300039. ...Was he killed?``
``...It made the newspaper, I think... maybe just the local ones.@k@vS14/11/210700052. ...Yes, the following year, he was murdered in a fight with his workers.``
``...After being killed in a gruesome manner, the body will be torn apart and discarded.``
``Was the body... mutilated?``
``You already knew?@k@vS14/11/210700054. ...That's exactly right. His right arm was never found.@k@vS14/11/210700055. Wait, how do you know about this, Akasaka-san?``
``...The following year, on that same day in June, 1980.@k@vS14/27/100500186. ...Satoko's parents will be pushed to their deaths.``
``...Perhaps it should be called an accident.@k@vS14/27/100500188. ...An unfortunate accident.``
``Let me continue confirming.@k@vS14/13/211300043. The year after that. The year following the foreman's murder.``
``...I believe it was... let me think... that's right.@k@vS14/13/211300045. Satoko-san, it was. I think she was a woman.@k@vS14/13/211300046. Something happened to her parents...``

``My, you're well informed.@k@vS14/11/210700057. That's correct. The Houjou couple, who supported the dam, fell from an observation deck while on vacation.@k@vS14/11/210700058. How do you know about this, Akasaka-san?``
``And then, the year after that, on that same day in June, 1981.@k@vS14/27/100500190. ...My parents will be killed.``
``...And the year after that...``
``Yes. Furude Rika's parents died.@k@vS14/11/210700060. ...But those incidents never quite sat right with me.``

``You sure know a lot about the curse of Oyashiro-sama.@k@vS14/00/213000012. These cases were suppressed by local pressure and never made public.@k@vS14/00/213000013. Are you from here, by any chance?``
``......What have I... done...``
``But even for you, the next year should be impossible to know about.@k@vS14/00/213000015. Starting from the fourth year, it was classified as a covert investigation.``
``...No... I think I know about that one too.``
``And the following year, on that same day in June, 1982.@k@vS14/27/100500192. Satoko's cruel aunt will have her head split open and die.``
``The year after that would be last year.@k@vS14/13/211300051. ...Would it happen that Satoko-san's aunt was killed by blunt force to the head?@k@vS14/13/211300052. ...Was that how it happened?``
``Y-yes... that's right on the mark.@k@vS14/11/210700062. Akasaka-san, how do you know all of this?``
``......I think... I also know who will be killed the year after that.``
``Wh-what are you saying?@k@vS14/00/213000017. The year after last year... that's this year, you realize...``
``...I will be killed.``
``June, 1983.@k@vS14/13/211300054. In other words, this very month.@k@vS14/13/211300055. ...Furude Rika will be murdered.``
Ooishi and the others sat in stunned silence.
Akasaka had described four consecutive mysterious deaths.
While he had been stationed far away, this was a man who belonged to one of the most specialized units in Public Safety.
Even with the investigations classified as covert, it wouldn't have been impossible for him to learn about them.
...Which was why Ooishi didn't find it too strange that Akasaka, despite being away from Hinamizawa for years, could recount the incidents one after another.

But as Akasaka grew visibly shaken by the accuracy of his own words, and then named this year's victim of the curse -- someone he couldn't possibly have known -- their drunkenness evaporated in an instant.
changeReason: Old truncates the detail about Akasaka's bewildered expression at the accuracy of his own words, but preserves the core meaning about naming the victim and sobering up.

TextENGNew: ``Akasaka-san... it's getting quite late.@k@vS14/11/210700064. We're all exhausted, so let's cut to the chase.@k@vS14/11/210700065. Where did you hear all of this?``
``Right.@k@vS14/13/211300057. Everything I just told you is what she told me when I visited Hinamizawa in June of 1978.``
``......What did you say...?``
Ooishi had meant to follow up with 'That's absurd,' but... the shock was so great that no further words came from his gaping mouth.
changeReason: OLD truncates to generic speechlessness; JPN and NEW include the detail of intending to say "That's absurd."

TextENGNew: ``I understand it's hard to believe.@k@vS14/13/211300059. When she told me back then, I dismissed it as a little girl playing at prophecy.@k@vS14/13/211300060. ...I never shared it with anyone.``

``What she was really doing, I think... was sending me a clear signal that she needed help.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1019. ...If that's the case, I... I absolutely want to confirm it.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1020. Whether even now, she... truly needs my help...``
Hearing Akasaka's words... Ooishi and the others fell into heavy silence.
It was an incredibly difficult story to accept... but Akasaka's face showed neither drunkenness nor any hint that he was joking.
After exhaling a long, heavy breath that had been building in his chest, Ooishi turned to face Akasaka directly.

``...Akasaka-san, I'm retiring this year.@k@vS14/11/210700068. I want to avenge my friend who was killed in the first year, before my time is up.@k@vS14/11/210700069. He was a senpai who taught me everything I know.``
``That's why I'll pursue every lead, no matter how small or dubious.``
``...So let me just confirm one thing.@k@vS14/11/210700071. What you just said -- it wasn't a joke, was it?@k@vS14/11/210700072. It wasn't some tall tale spun to amuse an old man?``

``I've told you nothing but the truth.@k@vS14/13/211300064. Furude Rika spoke those words to me in June of 1978.@k@vS14/13/211300065. Whether it was a prophecy or something else, I can't say. But every word she said was real.``

``N-no, that's understandable.@k@vS14/00/213000019. Even if you'd told us, we wouldn't have been able to take it at face value.@k@vS14/00/213000020. Four deaths ago -- that's just not something you can act on.``
Ooishi must have been harboring the bitter thought that if only Akasaka had told him sooner, his friend's death might have been prevented.
...But the old forensic investigator was right. Even if Akasaka had told him back then, Ooishi wouldn't have believed it.
And his friend would have died all the same, leaving Ooishi with nothing but the regret of having failed to act.
``...You're right.@k@vS14/11/210700074. No -- I'm the one who should apologize.@k@vS14/11/210700075. ...Still... Furude Rika.@k@vS14/11/210700076. That little girl knew, even back then...?``
``I don't have detailed knowledge of the current situation in Hinamizawa.``
``I did read in the newspaper that the dam construction plan was shelved indefinitely.@k@vS14/13/211300069. So I'd assumed the dam conflict was over and peace had returned...``

``Hmm. The dam conflict is indeed over.@k@vS14/00/213000022. ...But starting the very next year, these so-called 'Oyashiro-sama's curse' incidents began occurring annually.@k@vS14/00/213000023. Without exception.``
``...And they played out exactly as you described.``

``...It seems she entrusted me with something of enormous importance.@k@vS14/13/211300071. ...Ooishi-san, let me ask you directly.@k@vS14/13/211300072. Is there any possibility that Furude Rika herself might be targeted this year?``

``...The Furude couple was reportedly looked down upon for being passive during the dam conflict.@k@vS14/11/210700078. That's why people said they were struck by the curse in the third year.@k@vS14/11/210700079. There's no guarantee the same thing won't happen to their daughter.``
``...It's possible, then?``
Ooishi nodded slowly, wordlessly.
``...Tomorrow, I'll try to make contact with Furude Rika.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1022. I feel bad about Yukie and the others, but... it seems I need to speak with her directly.``
``Akasaka-san... if what you say is true, would you let me in on this?``
``...I think it would be counterproductive for me to come along right now, so I'll leave it in your hands. But whatever Furude Rika has to say is going to be critical for me as well.``
changeReason: OLD mistranslates; JPN says Ooishi thinks his presence would be counterproductive and is delegating, not discussing desires.

TextENGNew: ``...My, my... you've brought us quite the extraordinary gift...``
``I'm getting rather intrigued myself.@k@vS14/00/213000026. I never thought I'd witness the solving of a mystery I assumed would end in smoke.``

``However, I do have a question.@k@vS14/13/211300077. Why would she confide something this important only to me?@k@vS14/13/211300078. If she wanted help, it should have been the local police, not someone on a business trip from Tokyo.``

``...Hmmm.@k@vS14/11/210700083. ...Probably because she couldn't fully trust the local police.@k@vS14/11/210700084. I'm a rough sort of man myself.@k@vS14/11/210700085. Perhaps I hadn't earned Rika-san's trust.``
``...Besides, a fair number of people with ties to the Sonozaki family have infiltrated the Okinomiya precinct.``
``So in other words, she trusted me -- the outsider from Tokyo -- more than anyone else...?``
``As much as it stings to admit, that's probably the case.``
changeReason: OLD truncates 悔しいですが to just "Yeah, I guess," losing the emotional reluctance of Ooishi's admission.

TextENGNew: ``...In that case, Ooishi-san.@k@vS14/13/211300081. Let's keep this strictly between us.``
``Absolutely.@k@vS14/11/210700089. ...At any rate, tomorrow, could you try to meet with Rika-san in Hinamizawa?@k@vS14/11/210700090. And please, keep me informed.``
